title: Narrative Arc — The No Idle State Rule
recognition: Execution systems often stop because they lose state, return to conversational posture, or ask for confirmation when the next task is already known.
reframe: After execution is authorized, waiting without a genuine blocker is an operating failure rather than caution.
framework: Select the highest-priority unblocked task, execute it, verify it, update state, and continue until completion or a valid stop condition.
proof: The rule is codified in repository governance and reflected in the system's queues, gates, state index, fallback adapters, and verification loop.
application: Use the rule in AI-assisted operations, project governance, transformation work, and any system where approved work spans multiple steps.
consequence: State-backed execution reduces repeated planning, tool rediscovery, unnecessary confirmation, and premature summaries.
invitation: When the next task is known and safe, execute it rather than converting the workflow back into a conversation.
